.ddx/executions/20260505T193318-22288952/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
Review sessions have no cost cap enforcement, no reviewer routing policy, and no structured refusal contracts. Without these, a review session can run unbounded cost, use an unavailable reviewer, or fail with an unstructured error that the frontend cannot surface to the operator.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/review_session.go (from ddx-e7b80a50, a dep): ReviewSession has max_billable_usd field but no enforcement code.
- No reviewer routing logic exists in the server review path — preferred_reviewer from Story 17 and the escalation ladder from Story 10 are not consulted.
- No structured refusal error codes exist (PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE).
- ddx-0e5c3005 (reviewer routing contract, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Enforce max_billable_usd per session: before each turn, check cumulative cost_usd against max_billable_usd; if exceeded, return COST_CAP_EXCEEDED refusal.
- Reviewer routing: consult Story 17's preferred_reviewer first; fall back to Story 10's escalation ladder.
- Downgrade routing: when preferred reviewer is unavailable, downgrade to next ladder rung and emit review-pairing-degraded event.
- Implement three structured refusal error codes as typed errors with structured JSON body.

NON-SCOPE
- Turn dispatcher implementation (ddx-f7c3d512, a dep).
- Frontend display of refusal codes (handled by review panel bead).
.ddx/executions/20260505T193318-22288952/prompt.md:11:1. max_billable_usd enforced per session: turn rejected with COST_CAP_EXCEEDED when cumulative cost_usd would exceed limit.
2. Reviewer routing consults Story 17 preferred_reviewer first, then Story 10 escalation ladder.
3. Downgrade routing: unavailable preferred reviewer falls back to next ladder rung with review-pairing-degraded event.
4. Three refusal codes implemented: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE.
5. TestReview_CostCap_ReturnsCostCapExceeded verifies enforcement at max_billable_usd.
6. TestReview_ReviewerUnavailable_Downgrades verifies downgrade path and event.
7. TestReview_RefusalCodes_HaveStructuredBody verifies all three codes return JSON body.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:31:{"acceptance":"1. New cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go defines ReviewVerdict struct with Verdict (string, enum-validated), Findings ([]Finding), Summary (string), and SchemaVersion (int) fields.\n2. ParseReviewVerdict accepts: raw JSON; ```json …``` fenced JSON; mixed prose+JSON where the JSON is the last fenced block. Rejects: unknown verdict values; missing verdict field; truncated JSON.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestParseReviewVerdict ./internal/agent/... passes with one subtest per accepted/rejected case above.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerEmitsJSONContract ./internal/agent/... passes: uses a fixture reviewer transcript with '### Verdict: APPROVE' wrapped markdown and asserts the extractor call site rejects it with the 'unparseable' class — confirming the markdown path is gone.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerJSONHappyPath ./internal/agent/... passes: a fixture transcript containing the JSON contract parses to a ReviewVerdict with verdict=APPROVE.\n6. The reviewer prompt template (in execute_bead_review.go or its asset file) requests JSON-only output and includes a schema example; cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewPromptRequestsJSON ./internal/agent/... passes by string-matching the template.\n7. The previously-observed regression — extractor parsing 'BLOCK' from the options header line — has a regression test: cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerNoOptionsHeaderRegression ./internal/agent/... feeds a transcript that includes only the prompt template's options header echoed back and asserts the parser rejects it (unparseable), not parses it as BLOCK.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:24:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"ed2219bf951ce9b7770ed684fb6c789a51b3344d","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:51.532484284Z","description":"Upstream report (\"ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts\", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.\n\nFEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.\n\nSwitch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:\n\n  {\n    \"verdict\": \"APPROVE\" | \"REQUEST_CHANGES\" | \"BLOCK\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      { \"severity\": \"info\"|\"warn\"|\"block\", \"summary\": \"…\", \"location\": \"path:line?\" }\n    ],\n    \"summary\": \"≤300 char human-readable verdict justification\"\n  }\n\nReplace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:\n1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.\n2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.\n3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.\n4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)\n- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated\n\nOut of scope:\n- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)\n- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)\n- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)\n\nCross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).","events_attachment":"ddx-021bd69b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-25T09:39:11.049944919Z","id":"ddx-021bd69b","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:review"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-b2bae304","status":"closed","title":"review: machine-readable JSON verdict contract + replace markdown extractor","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:40:01.996055932Z"}

Provide shared primitives every prompt-building call site must use,\ncollapse the duplicate review assembler, bound review/grading evidence\nassembly, hard-fail oversize prompt ingress, bound all server text\negress, add section-level assembly telemetry, and classify review\nfailures with a bounded retry policy.\n\nOriginal bug that motivated this work: post-merge reviewer silently\nreturns zero bytes on beads with large diffs, causing the primary task\nto be re-executed 3–4 times and burning tokens. Root cause: reviewer\nprompt assembly inlines full governing docs and full git show diff\nwithout a cap; oversize prompts trigger provider-side failures that\nDDx classifies as retryable.\n\nChild beads implement Stages A through G per the spec's Delivery\nSequencing section. The `git show`/`git diff` call that produces the \u003cdiff\u003e section of a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt passes a pathspec that excludes `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/*`, `.ddx/executions/*/prompt.md`, `.ddx/executions/*/usage.json`, and `.ddx/executions/*/manifest.json`. Verified by reading the updated implementations in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:gitShow` and `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:beadReviewGitShow` — both invoke `git show \u003crev\u003e -- . ':(exclude)...'` (or functional equivalent) with the documented exclusion set.\n\n2. Regression test: a unit test builds a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt against a synthetic evidence commit whose diff adds a `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/embedded/agent-fake.jsonl` containing \u003e=20,000 `+`-prefixed lines. The test asserts the resulting prompt is below a fixed token budget (suggest 150k tokens, ~600k bytes) and does NOT contain any line matching `agent-fake.jsonl`.\n\n3. Token-budget guard: a unit test asserts that when a `\u003cbead-review\u003e` prompt would exceed 150k tokens even after the exclusion, the builder elides to a summary stub (per suggested fix #2) and logs a warning rather than emitting the full content.\n\n4. Execution-evidence staging exclusion: `landEvidence` (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go`) and `VerifyCleanWorktree` (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:VerifyCleanWorktree`) stage the evidence directory with a pathspec that excludes `embedded/` (matching the existing `SynthesizeCommit` exclusion at `execute_bead.go:269-283`), OR move raw session logs out of the tracked evidence directory entirely (suggested fix #3). Covered by a regression test that runs a complete execute-bead attempt end-to-end in a temporary repo and asserts `git log -p --follow '.ddx/executions/*/embedded/*.jsonl'` returns nothing (no tracked session log).\n\n5. End-to-end reproduction of the `hx-dd4ab35f`-style scenario: an integration test simulates the nexiq sequence (attempt lands impl commit, evidence commit adds 20k-line session log, review-malfunction triggers retry) and asserts the retry's synthesized `prompt.md` is under the budget cap AND the review call succeeds against a stub provider without an n_keep/n_ctx error.\n\n6. Existing execution evidence continues to satisfy auditability: either still tracked but excluded from review diffs, or moved to a dedicated ref / ignored store per the chosen fix. `ddx agent metrics` and `ddx bead metrics` still surface per-attempt usage/tokens/cost from the evidence bundle after the fix.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T00:13:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1551897","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:08:23.667089738Z","description":"## Summary\n\nOn retry, DDx synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt whose \u003cdiff\u003e section is the output of `git show \u003cresultRev\u003e`. When the prior attempt's landing sequence appended an evidence commit of the form `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt_id\u003e]` that added `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt_id\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` (full per-event NDJSON session log) as a tracked file, `resultRev` is that evidence commit's SHA (because `landEvidence` sets `result.NewTip = sha` and the orchestrator copies `land.NewTip` into `res.ResultRev`). `git show` of the evidence commit emits the entire session log diff with a `+` prefix per line. Session logs are 5-50k lines; the retry prompt exceeds every public context window.\n\n## Category / severity / surface\n\n- Category: correctness / resource-exhaustion\n- Severity: high\n- Surface: `ddx agent execute-bead` retry path triggered by `kind:review-malfunction` (and any other flow that re-synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt from a git diff/show since base_rev).\n\n## Full bug description (verbatim)\n\nOn retry, DDx synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt whose \u003cdiff\u003e section is the raw `git diff \u003cbase_rev\u003e..\u003cresult_rev\u003e` of the prior attempt. The prior attempt's landing sequence includes an evidence commit of the form:\n\n    chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt_id\u003e]\n\nthat adds `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt_id\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` — the full per-event NDJSON session log of the prior attempt — as a tracked file. Because that file is *added* in the diff range, the diff includes it in full with a `+` prefix on every line. Session logs are routinely 5-50k lines. One reasonable fix iteration is enough to push the retry prompt past 1-2 million tokens, which no currently supported model can accept — the provider streams back an n_keep \u003e n_ctx error and the retry crashes without a request round-trip.\n\nThis is a self-poisoning loop: the \"evidence\" DDx commits to make attempts auditable is the exact artifact that makes the next attempt undeliverable.\n\n## Reproduction (observed live 2026-04-17 in nexiq)\n\n1. Bead `hx-dd4ab35f` claimed by execute-loop worker (harness=agent, model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, provider=vidar).\n2. Attempt `20260417T211600-d6a7dee6` succeeded and landed two commits: `cb5e733` (implementation + regression test [hx-dd4ab35f]) and `9a006dd` (chore: add execution evidence [20260417T211600-], adds `.ddx/executions/20260417T211600-d6a7dee6/embedded/agent-1776460560227794836.jsonl`, +20,623 lines).\n3. Post-merge review emitted `BLOCK` without a rationale; DDx recorded `kind:review-malfunction` and reopened the bead for retry.\n4. Retry `20260417T212928-27f9054a` synthesized `.ddx/executions/20260417T212928-27f9054a/prompt.md` of 6,307,997 bytes; `\u003cbead-review\u003e` block = 6,307,969 bytes; 99.9998% is the diff of the prior evidence commit, 99%+ of that diff is the session log file. Rest of \u003cbead-review\u003e (title/description/acceptance/labels/governing) is ~28 bytes.\n5. lmstudio responded: \"n_keep: 2,274,074 \u003e= n_ctx: 196,608\". Worker recorded failed attempt with zero usage. Same pattern on vidar (n_keep: 1,169,147) and bragi (n_keep: 2,274,074), all running qwen3.6-35b-a3b at max_context_length 262144. Provider/model is irrelevant — prompt exceeds every public context window.\n\n## DDx code-path verification (Claude investigation 2026-04-17)\n\nRelevant files and line ranges in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:553-561` — `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow(rev)` runs `git -C \u003cprojectRoot\u003e show \u003crev\u003e` with no pathspec. Output is injected raw into `\u003cdiff\u003e` by `BuildReviewPrompt` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:383-384`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:427-431` — reviewer fetches `diff := r.gitShow(resultRev)` then builds the prompt.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:540-555` (`landEvidence`) — creates `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cshort\u003e]` and sets `result.NewTip = sha`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:428-434` — `RealLandingGitOps.StageDir` runs `git add -- \u003crelPath\u003e` on the entire `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/` directory with no exclusions for `embedded/`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:250-251` — after Land, `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`, so `ResultRev` ends up pointing at the evidence commit. The reviewer then runs `git show \u003cevidence-commit\u003e`, which is literally the dump of the session log.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1135-1164` (`VerifyCleanWorktree`) — also stages the evidence dir via `git add -- \u003cevidenceDir\u003e` and commits `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cshort\u003e]`; same no-exclusion pattern.\n- `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171` (`beadReviewGitShow`) — mirror of the same bug in the standalone `ddx bead review` command.\n\nConfirmation the bug reporter's claim about `:(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded`:\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` (`SynthesizeCommit`) — the exclusion pathspec IS present here; the bug is that the evidence-landing commit path (`StageDir` + `VerifyCleanWorktree`) and the review path (`git show \u003crev\u003e`) do not share this exclusion.\n- `cli/cmd/init.go:19-46` — the gitignore rules DO include `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/`, but `git add -- \u003cdir\u003e` on pre-existing or explicitly-added paths can still stage, and older workspaces may pre-date this gitignore (matches nexiq's scenario where the evidence commit landed the session log).\n\n## Deviation from bug description (minor)\n\nThe bug text describes the diff as `git diff \u003cbase_rev\u003e..\u003cresult_rev\u003e`. The code actually does `git show \u003cresultRev\u003e` (single-rev show). The self-poisoning mechanism is functionally identical: when `resultRev` points at the evidence commit (`landEvidence` + `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`), `git show` emits the entire `+`-prefixed session log. All suggested fixes still apply.\n\n## Suggested fixes (ascending scope)\n\n1. Exclude execution-evidence paths from bead-review diffs by passing a pathspec to the `git show`/`git diff` call in the prompt builder: `-- . ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded/*' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/usage.json'`. The same exclusion already exists at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` for `SynthesizeCommit`; replicate it in `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow` (`execute_bead_review.go:553-561`), `beadReviewGitShow` (`cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171`), `landEvidence` → `StageDir` (`execute_bead_land.go:428-434,540-555`), and `VerifyCleanWorktree` (`execute_bead.go:1135-1164`).\n2. Substitute, don't reproduce. When a tracked-file addition exceeds a byte/line budget in the diff, replace it with a one-line stub such as: `+++ b/.ddx/executions/.../agent-*.jsonl  (+20,623 lines of session evidence — omitted from review prompt)`.\n3. Stop committing raw session logs as tracked evidence. Move `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/*.jsonl` to a gitignored store, or a separate `refs/ddx/evidence/*` ref analogous to `refs/ddx/iterations/*`.\n4. Cap the \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt size with a hard budget (e.g., 150k tokens). Elide to summary + log warning when exceeded.\n\n(1) is the minimal surgical fix; (3) is the correct long-term fix.\n\n## Evidence in nexiq repo\n\n- Bloated retry prompt: `.ddx/executions/20260417T212928-27f9054a/prompt.md` (6,307,997 bytes; \u003cbead-review\u003e body = 6,307,969 bytes).\n- Successful prior attempt: `.ddx/executions/20260417T211600-d6a7dee6/` (landed cb5e733 + 9a006dd).\n- Failing agent session: `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-1776460487823541600.jsonl` (session.end payload has the n_keep/n_ctx error verbatim).\n- Bead event trail: `ddx bead show hx-dd4ab35f` — routing, then `kind:review-malfunction summary:BLOCK without rationale`, then `kind:execute-bead summary:review_malfunction`.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T00:13:24.428420423Z","id":"ddx-39e27896","issue_type":"bug","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:bug","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:prompt-synthesis"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead retry prompt explodes when prior attempt committed session-log evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-18T00:29:15.347462806Z"}

New regression test cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestExecuteLoopRetryReusesWorkerHarness asserts: spawn execute-loop with --harness claude; bead reaches REQUEST_CHANGES → reopen → next iteration's service.Execute request has Harness=\"claude\" (not empty).\n2. Same test covers --model, --profile, and --provider propagation across review-driven reopens.\n3. Test covers all reopen sources (review BLOCK, review REQUEST_CHANGES, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed, no_changes if it triggers reopen) — every reopen path must propagate pins.\n4. Negative test: worker spawned without --harness; reopen retry continues to use auto-routing (no propagation regression).\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... -count=1 passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"82902a25231cfae083a385eed40195398768cb22","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:22:06.953399219Z","description":"Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.\n\n## Symptom\n\nWorker spawned with explicit pin:\n\n    ddx work --harness claude\n\nFirst attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:\n\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted\n\nNote harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness=\"\". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.\n\nConcrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.\n\n## Principle\n\n**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.\n\nThis is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.\n\n## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)\n\nThe agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.\n\nThe fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path\n- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct\n- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)\n- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)","events_attachment":"ddx-c67969d5/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent: execute: model \"Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit\" is not supported by harness \"pi\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-pro","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-26T05:34:55Z","id":"ddx-c67969d5","issue_type":"bug","labels":["ddx","kind:bug","area:agent","area:execute-loop","area:retry"],"priority":1,"session_id":"eb-5a4a01b7","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: reopen-retry must reuse initial worker params (harness, model, profile)","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:17:11.36109409Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:828:{"acceptance":"(1) Reviewer verdict extraction consumes NORMALIZED reviewer text (the harness's final cleaned response), not raw stream frames. If the agent module does not yet expose a normalized final-response API, this bead is blocked on the agent-side stream normalizer bead (sibling in ~/Projects/agent).\n(2) Fixture reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) drives a regression test: the pre-fix behavior records BLOCK; the fix records APPROVE.\n(3) ParseReviewVerdict no longer defaults to BLOCK on an unrecognized verdict line. Unrecognized output returns a parse error; the caller handles that as a review-error (feeds ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path), not a silent BLOCK.\n(4) Unit tests cover: clean APPROVE, clean BLOCK, REQUEST_CHANGES, unparseable, empty.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:55:04.187171368Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f7ae036f","depends_on_id":"ddx-004eccbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:04:11Z"}],"description":"## Wave 2 framing: risk-reduction, not correctness\n\nThis bead (scope:partial) stops silent-BLOCK mis-records — it does NOT stop mis-extraction itself. After wave:2 lands, the reviewer will still misfire on codex/claude raw stream frames because clause (1) (consume normalized final text) is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5. What changes: misfires surface as loud review-error reopens instead of silent BLOCKs, feeding ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path. Expect a \"review-error reopens per closed bead\" metric to look rough in this window. The real extraction fix lands when agent-32e8ff5e ships upstream and ddx-7bc0c8d5 migrates.\n\n---\n\nReporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 1 of review-malfunction report.\n\nThe post-merge reviewer in `ddx agent execute-loop` records `{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}` in `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/result.json` even when the reviewer LLM produced a well-formed `### Verdict: APPROVE` (or REQUEST_CHANGES) with full AC grade table and cited evidence. Observed ~20 times in a row on nexiq codex+claude loop 2026-04-19 against beads nexiq-b5661a77, nexiq-50485cd3, nexiq-be1bc61d, nexiq-8999acab, hx-8a26c6df, hx-4901d83a, hx-e33210f6, nexiq-35d64a6b, and more — every bead in the run.\n\nCode site: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:76` — `reVerdictLine` regex `^#{1,4}\\s+Verdict:\\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\\s*$`. When the reviewer output contains the raw verdict on a heading line but is wrapped inside a codex streaming JSON event body (e.g. `{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{...\"text\":\"### Verdict: APPROVE...\"}}` with escaped newlines), the regex does not match across the wrapped payload and `ParseReviewVerdict` falls through to the `VerdictBlock` default at line 86 (\"safe default: requires human attention\"). The bead then gets a `review_malfunction` event with detail \"post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)\".\n\nActual reviewer output is preserved in `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` under the reviewer session id and can be recovered post-facto; the parser just fails to see it.\n\nHypothesis from report: parser anchors on `### Verdict:` heading but a codex harness stream serializes text with `\\n` escaping or wraps the reviewer body inside thread/turn JSON events so the multiline anchor never hits. Root fix options: (a) extract reviewer text from the raw stream before parsing (strip codex thread/turn JSON to the inner `item_0.text` payload); (b) switch the reviewer prompt to require machine-readable JSON `{verdict, ac_grades, summary}` and parse that; (c) broaden the regex to match non-anchored `### Verdict: X` headings even inside JSON-escaped text.\n\n## Upstream dependency\n\nBlocked on agent-32e8ff5e (in ~/Projects/agent): the fix moves reviewer-text extraction behind the ddx-agent service boundary so DDx no longer parses raw harness stream frames. Until that lands, f7ae036f's clause (1) on \"normalized reviewer text\" cannot be satisfied and the regression test from ddx-004eccbc remains red. The parse-error / no-default-BLOCK changes (clauses 3-4) can land independently and are worth doing now.\n\n## Scope split (wave 2)\n\nClauses (3) and (4) — no default-BLOCK, parse errors surface as review-error, fixture regression test — land in wave 2 with only ddx-004eccbc as a prereq.\n\nClause (1) — consume normalized final text from the agent service instead of raw stream frames — is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5 because it depends on agent-32e8ff5e shipping upstream. This bead is labeled scope:partial to signal that wave 2 only closes (3)+(4); a follow-up pass on (1) happens inside ddx-7bc0c8d5's migration.","id":"ddx-f7ae036f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","kind:review","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer","scope:partial"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: post-merge reviewer mis-extracts verdict, emits spurious BLOCK on APPROVE","updated_at":"2026-04-20T15:50:37.451719605Z"}

It clamps governing docs and the diff, then emits one prompt blob and telemetry, but it does not model per-turn pinned/rolling sections or a dedicated refusal class for a minimum pinned floor that cannot fit. The review-session store in `cli/internal/server/review_session.go:22-224` persists the right inputs (`artifact_git_rev`, `template_ref`, `prompt_ref`, `turns.jsonl`) yet nothing consumes them in a structured prompt-rendering step.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- `cli/internal/server/review_session.go` or a new helper in the same package: add a prompt renderer that emits pinned sections for system rubric, artifact identity, Story 17 template+prompt, first-user-intent, and explicit user decisions, followed by rolling sections for session memory summary, last K verbatim turns, and unresolved findings.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go`: thread the structured renderer into the review path, keep FEAT-022 byte accounting on the assembled prompt, and short-circuit before provider dispatch when the minimum pinned floor does not fit. The refusal should surface `PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` with the observed bytes and cap in the error text.\n- Add tests named `TestReviewSessionPromptAssemblyPinnedRolling`, `TestReviewSessionPromptAssemblyBudgetRefusal`, and `TestReviewPromptTelemetryIncludesSectionBytes` to pin the section order, refusal behavior, and byte accounting.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change the existing review verdict parser or the review error taxonomy.\n- Do not add new server text-egress caps in this slice.\n- Do not rework the existing diff clamping primitives; this bead only consumes them.",

.ddx/executions/20260505T142253-f8474e12/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
The current review/session prompt path does not assemble a turn-structured prompt envelope with pinned evidence, rolling session memory, and explicit refusal on minimum-floor overflow. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:204-333` only builds a single-shot review prompt around bead metadata, governing docs, and diff. `cli/internal/server/review_session.go:22-224` stores artifact identity, template refs, prompt refs, and append-only turns, but there is no renderer that turns that session state into a structured prompt with pinned + rolling sections.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:204-333` is the current bounded review assembler. It clamps governing docs and the diff, then emits one prompt blob and telemetry, but it does not model per-turn pinned/rolling sections or a dedicated refusal class for a minimum pinned floor that cannot fit. The review-session store in `cli/internal/server/review_session.go:22-224` persists the right inputs (`artifact_git_rev`, `template_ref`, `prompt_ref`, `turns.jsonl`) yet nothing consumes them in a structured prompt-rendering step.

PROPOSED FIX
- `cli/internal/server/review_session.go` or a new helper in the same package: add a prompt renderer that emits pinned sections for system rubric, artifact identity, Story 17 template+prompt, first-user-intent, and explicit user decisions, followed by rolling sections for session memory summary, last K verbatim turns, and unresolved findings.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go`: thread the structured renderer into the review path, keep FEAT-022 byte accounting on the assembled prompt, and short-circuit before provider dispatch when the minimum pinned floor does not fit. The refusal should surface `PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` with the observed bytes and cap in the error text.
- Add tests named `TestReviewSessionPromptAssemblyPinnedRolling`, `TestReviewSessionPromptAssemblyBudgetRefusal`, and `TestReviewPromptTelemetryIncludesSectionBytes` to pin the section order, refusal behavior, and byte accounting.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the existing review verdict parser or the review error taxonomy.
- Do not add new server text-egress caps in this slice.
- Do not rework the existing diff clamping primitives; this bead only consumes them.
.ddx/executions/20260505T142253-f8474e12/prompt.md:11:1. A structured prompt renderer exists and emits pinned sections, rolling sections, and unresolved findings in that order.
2. The review path records FEAT-022 byte accounting for the assembled prompt and the emitted artifact bundle.
3. When the minimum pinned floor cannot fit, the caller refuses with `PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` before provider dispatch.
4. `TestReviewSessionPromptAssemblyPinnedRolling`, `TestReviewSessionPromptAssemblyBudgetRefusal`, and `TestReviewPromptTelemetryIncludesSectionBytes` cover the new behavior.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/executions/20260505T143454-88ed54dc/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM\nPrompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThere is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.\n- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.\n- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.\n- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.\n- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.",

.ddx/executions/20260505T143454-88ed54dc/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! Adds tables for:\\n4\\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\\n5\\t//! - users: global user identities\\n6\\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\\n7\\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\\n8\\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\\n9\\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\\n10\\t//!\\n11\\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here — it\\n12\\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\\n13\\t\\n14\\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\\n15\\t///\\n16\\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\\n17\\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\\n18\\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: \u0026rusqlite::Connection) -\u003e Result\u003c(), String\u003e {\\n19\\t    // Create tenants table.\\n20\\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\\n21\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n22\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\\n23\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n24\\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\\n25\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n26\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n27\\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\\n28\\t        )\\\",\\n29\\t    )\\n30\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenants table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n31\\t\\n32\\t    // Create users table.\\n33\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n34\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\\n35\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n36\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n37\\t            email              TEXT,\\n38\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n39\\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\\n40\\t        )\\\",\\n41\\t    )\\n42\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n43\\t\\n44\\t    // Create user_identities table.\\n45\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\\n46\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n47\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\\n48\\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n49\\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n50\\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n51\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n52\\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\\n53\\t        )\\\",\\n54\\t    )\\n55\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"user_identities table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n56\\t\\n57\\t    // Create tenant_users table.\\n58\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\\n59\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n60\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\\n61\\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n62\\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n63\\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\\n64\\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n65\\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\\n66\\t        )\\\",\\n67\\t    )\\n68\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n69\\t\\n70\\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\\n71\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n72\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\\n73\\t            tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n74\\t            database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\\n75\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n76\\t            PRIMARY KEY Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\\n1\\t//!\\n2\\t//! # Layers\\n3\\t//!\\n4\\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\\n5\\t//!\\n6\\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`) control access to\\n7\\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\\n8\\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\\n9\\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\\n10\\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\\n11\\t//!\\n12\\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\\n15\\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\\n16\\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\\n17\\t//!\\n18\\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\\n21\\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\\n22\\t//!\\n23\\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\\n24\\t//!\\n25\\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\\\"*\\\"` for all databases).\\n26\\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\\n27\\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\\n28\\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\\n29\\t\\n30\\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\n31\\t\\n32\\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\\n33\\t\\n34\\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\\n35\\t///\\n36\\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`.\\n37\\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\\n38\\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\\n39\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n40\\tpub enum Role {\\n41\\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\\n42\\t    None = 0,\\n43\\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\\n44\\t    Read = 1,\\n45\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\\n46\\t    Write = 2,\\n47\\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\\n48\\t    Admin = 3,\\n49\\t}\\n50\\t\\n51\\timpl Role {\\n52\\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\\n53\\t    ///\\n54\\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\\n55\\t    ///\\n56\\t    /// | Input | Role |\\n57\\t    /// |-------|------|\\n58\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-admin\\\"` / `\\\"tag:admin\\\"` / `\\\"admin\\\"` | `Admin` |\\n59\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-write\\\"` / `\\\"tag:axon-agent\\\"` / `\\\"tag:write\\\"` / `\\\"write\\\"` | `Write` |\\n60\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-read\\\"` / `\\\"tag:read\\\"` / `\\\"read\\\"` | `Read` |\\n61\\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\\n62\\t    ///\\n63\\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\\n64\\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\\n65\\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: \u0026str) -\u003e Self {\\n66\\t        match tag {\\n67\\t            \\\"tag:axon-admin\\\" | \\\"tag:admin\\\" | \\\"admin\\\" =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n68\\t            \\\"tag:axon-write\\\" | \\\"tag:axon-agent\\\" | \\\"tag:write\\\" | \\\"write\\\" =\u003e Role::Write,\\n69\\t            \\\"tag:axon-read\\\" | \\\"tag:read\\\" | \\\"read\\\" =\u003e Role::Read,\\n70\\t            _ =\u003e Role::None,\\n71\\t        }\\n72\\t    }\\n73\\t\\n74\\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\\n75\\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: \u0026[String]) -\u003e Self {\\n76\\t        tags.iter()\\n77\\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\\n78\\t            .max()\\n79\\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\\n80\\t    }\\n81\\t}\\n82\\t\\n83\\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\\n84\\t    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {\\n85\\t        match self {\\n86\\t            Role::None =\u003e write!(f, \\\"none\\\"),\\n87\\t            Role::Read =\u003e write!(f, \\\"read\\\"),\\n88\\t            Role::Write =\u003e write!(f, \\\"write\\\"),\\n89\\t            Role::Admin =\u003e write!(f, \\\"admin\\\"),\\n90\\t        }\\n91\\t    }\\n92\\t}\\n93\\t\\n94\\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\\n95\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\\n96\\tpub enum Operation {\\n97\\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\\n98\\t    Read,\\n99\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\\n100\\t    Write,\\n101\\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\\n102\\t    Admin,\\n103\\t}\\n104\\t\\n105\\timpl Operation {\\n106\\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\\n107\\t    pub fn required_role(self) -\u003e Role {\\n108\\t        match self {\\n109\\t            Operation::Read =\u003e Role::Read,\\n110\\t            Operation::Write =\u003e Role::Write,\\n111\\t            Operation::Admin =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n112\\t        }\\n113\\t    }\\n114\\t}\\n115\\t\\n116\\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\\n117\\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\\n118\\t///\\n119\\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\\n120\\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\\n121\\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\\n122\\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\\n123\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n124\\tpub struct EntityFilter {\\n125\\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\\n126\\t    pub field: String,\\n127\\t    /// The required value for that field.\\n128\\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\\n129\\t}\\n130\\t\\n131\\timpl EntityFilter {\\n132\\t    /// Convenience constructor.\\n133\\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, value: serde_json::Value) -\u003e Self {\\n134\\t        Self {\\n135\\t            field: field.into(),\\n136\\t            value,\\n137\\t        }\\n138\\t    }\\n139\\t\\n140\\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\\n141\\t    ///\\n142\\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\\n143\\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\\n144\\t    pub fn matches(\u0026self, data: \u0026serde_json::Value) -\u003e bool {\\n145\\t        data.get(\u0026self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == \u0026self.value)\\n146\\t    }\\n147\\t}\\n148\\t\\n149\\t/// Identity context for a request.\\n150\\t///\\n151\\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\\n152\\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\\n153\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n154\\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\\n155\\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\\n156\\t    pub actor: String,\\n157\\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\\n158\\t    pub role: Role,\\n159\\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\\n160\\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\\n161\\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\\n162\\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \\\"Option::is_none\\\")]\\n163\\t    pub entity_filter: Option\u003cEntityFilter\u003e,\\n164\\t}\\n165\\t\\n166\\timpl CallerIdentity {\\n167\\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\\n168\\t    pub fn anonymous() -\u003e Self {\\n169\\t        Self {\\n170\\t            actor: \\\"anonymous\\\".into(),\\n171\\t            role: Role::Admin,\\n172\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n173\\t        }\\n174\\t    }\\n175\\t\\n176\\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\\n177\\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, role: Role) -\u003e Self {\\n178\\t        Self {\\n179\\t            actor: actor.into(),\\n180\\t            role,\\n181\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n182\\t        }\\n183\\t    }\\n184\\t\\n185\\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\\n186\\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -\u003e Self {\\n187\\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\\n188\\t        self\\n189\\t    }\\n190\\t\\n191\\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\\n192\\t    ///\\n193\\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\\n194\\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\\n195\\t    pub fn check(\u0026self, op: Operation) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n196\\t        let required = op.required_role();\\n197\\t        if self.role \u003e= required {\\n198\\t            Ok(())\\n199\\t        } else {\\n200\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n201\\t                \\\"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\\\",\\n202\\t                self.role, op, required\\n203\\t            )))\\n204\\t        }\\n205\\t    }\\n206\\t\\n207\\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\\n208\\t    ///\\n209\\t    /// Admin users see all fields. For other roles, fields listed in a\\n210\\t    /// mask policy that requires a higher role than the caller's are\\n211\\t    /// removed from the data.\\n212\\t    pub fn apply_masks(\u0026self, data: \u0026mut serde_json::Value, policies: \u0026[MaskPolicy]) {\\n213\\t        if self.role \u003e= Role::Admin {\\n214\\t            return; // Admin sees all.\\n215\\t        }\\n216\\t        if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {\\n217\\t            for policy in policies {\\n218\\t                if self.role \u003c policy.min_role {\\n219\\t                    obj.remove(\u0026policy.field);\\n220\\t                }\\n221\\t            }\\n222\\t        }\\n223\\t    }\\n224\\t}\\n225\\t\\n226\\t/// A field-level mask policy (US-046, FEAT-012).\\n227\\t///\\n228\\t/// Specifies that a field should be hidden from callers whose role is\\n229\\t/// below `min_role`. Admin always sees all fields.\\n230\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n231\\tpub struct MaskPolicy {\\n232\\t    /// The field name to mask.\\n233\\t    pub field: String,\\n234\\t    /// Minimum role required to see this field.\\n235\\t    pub min_role: Role,\\n236\\t}\\n237\\t\\n238\\t// ── Attribute-based write control (US-047, FEAT-012) ────────────────────────\\n239\\t\\n240\\t/// Per-collection write policy for attribute-based access control (ABAC).\\n241\\t///\\n242\\t/// Stored in the `__axon_policies__` pseudo-collection. Defines:\\n243\\t/// - Minimum role required to write to this collection\\n244\\t/// - Fields that are immutable after creation\\n245\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n246\\tpub struct WritePolicy {\\n247\\t    /// Collection this policy applies to.\\n248\\t    pub collection: String,\\n249\\t    /// Minimum role required for write operations.\\n250\\t    pub min_write_role: Role,\\n251\\t    /// Fields that cannot be modified after the initial create.\\n252\\t    #[serde(default)]\\n253\\t    pub immutable_fields: Vec\u003cString\u003e,\\n254\\t}\\n255\\t\\n256\\timpl WritePolicy {\\n257\\t    /// Check if a caller has sufficient role to write to this collection.\\n258\\t    pub fn check_write(\u0026self, caller: \u0026CallerIdentity) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n259\\t        if caller.role \u003e= self.min_write_role {\\n260\\t            Ok(())\\n261\\t        } else {\\n262\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n263\\t                \\\"write policy for '{}': role '{}' insufficient (requires '{}')\\\",\\n264\\t                self.collection, caller.role, self.min_write_role\\n265\\t            )))\\n266\\t        }\\n267\\t    }\\n268\\t\\n269\\t    /// Check if any immutable fields have been modified.\\n270\\t    ///\\n271\\t    /// Returns the list of field names that were illegally modified.\\n272\\t    pub fn check_immutable_fields(\\n273\\t        \u0026self,\\n274\\t        old_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n275\\t        new_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n276\\t    ) -\u003e Vec\u003cString\u003e {\\n277\\t        let mut violations = Vec::new();\\n278\\t        for field in \u0026self.immutable_fields {\\n279\\t            let old_val = old_data.get(field);\\n280\\t            let new_val = new_data.get(field);\\n281\\t            // If the field existed before and is now different or missing, it's a violation.\\n282\\t            if old_val.is_some() \u0026\u0026 old_val != new_val {\\n283\\t                violations.push(field.clone());\\n284\\t            }\\n285\\t        }\\n286\\t        violations\\n287\\t    }\\n288\\t}\\n289\\t\\n290\\t// ── Database-scoped access control (US-038, FEAT-014) ────────────────────────\\n291\\t\\n292\\t/// A grant that scopes a role to a specific database.\\n293\\t///\\n294\\t/// Without a grant, users cannot access a database (unless they have a\\n295\\t/// wildcard `*` grant or admin role).\\n296\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n297\\tpub struct DatabaseGrant {\\n298\\t    /// The actor (user/node) this grant applies to. `\\\"*\\\"` means all users.\\n299\\t    pub actor: String,\\n300\\t    /// The database name. `\\\"*\\\"` means all databases.\\n301\\t    pub database: String,\\n302\\t    /// The role granted for this database.\\n303\\t    pub role: Role,\\n304\\t}\\n305\\t\\n306\\t/// Registry of database-scoped grants.\\n307\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n308\\tpub struct GrantRegistry {\\n309\\t    grants: Vec\u003cDatabaseGrant\u003e,\\n310\\t}\\n311\\t\\n312\\timpl GrantRegistry {\\n313\\t    /// Create a new empty grant registry.\\n314\\t    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n315\\t        Self::default()\\n316\\t    }\\n317\\t\\n318\\t    /// Add a grant.\\n319\\t    pub fn add(\u0026mut self, grant: DatabaseGrant) {\\n320\\t        self.grants.push(grant);\\n321\\t .ddx/executions/20260425T233452-527fd4e5/manifest.json:15:    "description": "Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.\n\n## Symptom\n\nWorker spawned with explicit pin:\n\n    ddx work --harness claude\n\nFirst attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:\n\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted\n\nNote harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness=\"\". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.\n\nConcrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.\n\n## Principle\n\n**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.\n\nThis is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.\n\n## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)\n\nThe agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.\n\nThe fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path\n- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct\n- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)\n- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)",
.ddx/executions/20260425T233452-527fd4e5/prompt.md:5:Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.

## Symptom

Worker spawned with explicit pin:

    ddx work --harness claude

First attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:

    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted

Note harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness="". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.

Concrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.

## Principle

**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.

This is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.

## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)

The agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.

The fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.

## In-scope files

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path
- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer

## Out of scope

- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct
- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)
- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)
